sheets.name: 📊 Summary
sheets.title: 1080 Ideas— Quadrant 2
rows:
Passive, always-on browser overlay that automatically detects and flags suspicious content as the user browses. Uses visual indicators, smart filters, and notification systems to demote unverified claims and overlay context — without interrupting the reading experience.

WHY EASIEST TO BUILD: Pure frontend (browser extension). No user account needed. No complex backend. Overlay UI + lightweight rule-based or API-backed flag logic. MVP deliverable: a Chrome extension.

Active, on-demand fact-checking tool. The user highlights or clicks on a claim and receives instant debunks or cross-referenced sources drawn from trusted databases. Includes plugins and dashboards showing source credibility scores.

BUILD COMPLEXITY: Medium. Requires integration with a fact-checking API (e.g. ClaimBuster, Google Fact Check) and a source-credibility lookup service.

Engagement-driven education and reward layer. Gamified modules teach media literacy; a community-led reward system incentivises accurate reporting and slows reflexive sharing through friction prompts.

BUILD COMPLEXITY: Highest. Requires user accounts, a gamification engine, a points/badge persistence layer, and community moderation infrastructure.

sheets.name: 🟢 Group A – Warning Shield
sheets.title: Group A  ·  Passive Detection & Content Flagging  (205 ideas)
sheets.desc:
A Chrome/browser extension with a content-script overlay. Requires no user accounts, no persistent backend, and no third-party API contract. A working MVP can be built with HTML + CSS + JavaScript alone — flag suspicious phrasing patterns, overlay a visual badge, and surface a notification. All ideas in this group are achievable within a single sprint.

Gamified learning system that teaches media literacy, rewards accurate reporting, and introduces friction to slow down reflexive sharing of unverified content.
